data.title: ECB cuts key interest rates by 25 bps
data.description:
The European Central Bank cut its policy rates by 25 basis points, as expected, on Thursday, its third cut of the year, after policymakers flagged reduced inflation risks.

Markets now closely watching President Christine Lagarde's speech to gain more insights on the central bank's strategy.

 

ETFs: (EWG), (GF), (EWI), (EWQ), (FGM), (HEWG), (DAX), (FLFR), (FLGR), (FLIY)

Currency: (EUR:USD)

data.title: Allstate estimates September catastrophe losses at $889M, vs. $272M in August
data.description:
• Property and casualty insurer The Allstate (NYSE:ALL) estimated September catastrophe losses to come in at $889M, or $702M after-tax.
  • ALL's August estimated catastrophe losses stood at $272M, or $215M after-tax.
  • The September figure includes $630M, pre-tax, related to Hurricane Helene.
  • For Q3, catastrophe losses were $1.70B, or $1.35B after-tax.
  • For the first nine months of 2024, catastrophe losses were $4.55B or $3.60B, after-tax.
  • ALL shares were trading +0.18% Thursday pre-market at $196.01.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: ShiftPixy acquires TurboScale in $150M deal
data.description:
• ShiftPixy (NASDAQ:PIXY) said it has acquired AI tech company, TurboScale in a $150 million deal.
  • The deal consisted of $75 million in stock and $75 million in debt.
  • The acquisition will help ShiftPixy to introduce AI-powered features.
  • The company also expects accelerated growth and innovation after the acquisition, and is in plans to roll out new AI-driven tools by early 2025.

data.title: Lucid stock slides after pricing securities offering
data.description:
Shares of Lucid Group (NASDAQ:LCID) fell Thursday morning after the company priced its previously announced securities offering.

The offering will be conducted from time to time in one or more transactions. The automaker plans to sell 262.44M shares of its common stock and has granted the underwriter, BofA Securities, a 30-day option to purchase up to 39,367,040 additional shares of its common stock.

The company also said its majority stockholder, Ayar Third Investment Company, an affiliate of Saudi Arabia's Investment Fund, intends to purchase more than 374M shares in a private placement at the same price per share initially paid by the underwriter in the public offering. As a result of these purchases, Ayar expects to maintain its ~58.8% ownership of Lucid's outstanding common stock.

Lucid (LCID) expects to raise around $1.67B from the stock offering and the corresponding private placement. Net proceeds will be used for general corporate purposes, which may include, among other things, capital expenditures and working capital.

Shares of Lucid Group (LCID) fell 17% premarket on Thursday.

data.title: American Rebel regains compliance with Nasdaq
data.description:
• American Rebel (NASDAQ:AREB) said it has received a formal notice from the Nasdaq, stating that the company has regained compliance with the minimum bid price rule.
  • Press Release

data.title: SentinelOne investor day provides more confidence, Needham says
data.description:
• SentinelOne (NYSE:S) held its first-ever investor day, and research firm Needham left the event impressed on the company's execution and financial targets.
  • Shares rose 0.9% in premarket trading.
  • "We come away more confident in SentinelOne’s expanding platform, enhanced go-to-market, and improving execution," analyst Matt Dezort wrote in a note to clients.
  • Dezort, who has a Buy rating and $32 price target on SentinelOne, noted the day was "full of product announcements," including enhancements to Purple AI, updates to data and security information and event management, and the releases of Singularity Hyperautomation and Ultraviolet large language models. It also reiterated financial targets that it made when it went public and said cloud annual recurring revenue has topped $100M and data and security information and event management has topped $70M, above industry trends.
  • "Given Lenovo's leadership position in PC shipments, the new partnership could be transformational, and along with [SentinelOne's] improved positioning following the outage, could drive compelling upside to new ARR estimates over the coming years," Dezort added.

data.title: Wipro GAAP EPS of $0.07 in-line, revenue of $2.66B beats by $10M
data.description:
• Wipro press release (NYSE:WIT): Q2 GAAP EPS of $0.07 in-line.
  • Revenue of $2.66B (-1% Y/Y) beats by $10M.
  • Total bookings was at $3,561 million. Large deal bookings4 was at $1,489 million, an increaseof 28.8% QoQ and 16.8% YoY in constant currency
  • Voluntary attrition was at 14.5% on a trailing 12-month basis.
  • Wipro’s Board of Directors recommended issue of bonus shares to shareholders (includingstock dividend to ADS holders) in the ratio of 1:1 (1 equity share for every 1 equity share held),subject to approval of shareholders.

data.title: Nasdaq futures rise after robust results from TSMC revive confidence in tech stocks
data.description:
Stock index futures were higher on Thursday, with Nasdaq futures leading the pack, after strong results from Taiwan Semiconductor (TSM) helped revive investor sentiments.

S&P 500 futures (SPX) +0.4%, Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) +0.9% and Dow futures (INDU) +0.1%.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) rose 2 basis points to 4.04%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) was flat at 3.96%.

Wall Street on Wednesday ended higher, rebounding from a rout in chip stocks in the previous session. An ongoing rotation into small-caps and other sectors buoyed markets, while financials surged on a post-earnings pop in Morgan Stanley (MS).

"Markets put in a solid performance yesterday, with investors remaining upbeat as they digested several earnings results and another modest decline in oil prices," Deutsche Bank's Henry Allen said.

Chipmaker Taiwan Semiconductor (TSM) shares were up 7% in premarket trade, after it beat both its Q3 bottom and top line as it continued to benefit from the artificial intelligence boom, and helped traders renew their confidence in tech stocks.

On the macroeconomic side, the initial jobless claims and the September retail sales are both expected at the same time. The former is forecasted to fall to 241K, while the latter is seen to rise 0.3% on a monthly basis. Core retail sales is anticipated to come in at 0.1%.

The October Philly Fed business outlook will also land before the bell. The September industrial production and the Fed balance sheet will come later in the day.

"Manufacturing output (on the official data) has risen slightly this year. Sentiment (ISM, Philly Fed) has pointed to a near continuous contraction for the past two years. It is enough to make one question whether sentiment lives in the real world," UBS' Paul Donovan said.

data.title: Elevance Health stock slides after outlook cut amid rising medical expenses
data.description:
Shares of Elevance Health (NYSE:ELV) fell 12% Thursday morning after the health insurer lowered its forecast for full-year earnings amid higher-than-expected medical expenses in the third quarter.

The managed care player expects full year adjusted profit per share of around $33 (vs. prior guidance of at least $37.20), below consensus of $37.26.

On Tuesday, industry bellwether UnitedHealth (UNH) beat Wall Street estimates with its Q3 results, but cost concerns in its insurance unit unnerved Wall Street.

Elevance (ELV) reported adjusted profit of $8.37 per share for the third quarter, missing analysts’ expectations by $1.28, while its operating revenue of $44.7B grew +5.2% Y/Y and topped estimates.

The increase in revenue was “driven by higher premium yields in Health Benefits segment and growth in CarelonRx product revenue, partially offset by membership attrition in our Medicaid business.”

The company reported benefit expense ratio of 89.5% (estimate 87.6%), an improvement of 270 basis points, driven primarily by the timing mismatch between Medicaid rates and the higher acuity of its members. Total expenses surged 6.2% Y/Y to $43.73M.

CEO update: “We remain confident in the long-term earnings potential of our diverse businesses as we navigate a dynamic operating environment and unprecedented challenges in the Medicaid business. We expect Medicaid rates will align with the needs of our members in time, and are taking proactive actions to enhance operational efficiencies that will ensure we emerge from this period even stronger.”

Shares of peer insurers also dropped premarket: Molina Healthcare (MOH) -11.18%, Centene (CNC) -8%, CVS Health (CVS) -1.68%, Humana (HUM) -2.18%.

data.title: Schneider Electric to buy data center cooling firm Motivair in $850M deal
data.description:
Schneider Electric (OTCPK:SBGSF) (OTCPK:SBGSY) said Thursday it agreed to acquire a controlling interest in Buffalo-based Motivair for $850M in cash.

The deal will give Schneider (OTCPK:SBGSF) (OTCPK:SBGSY) a 75% stake of 75% in the company, which specializes in liquid cooling and advanced thermal management solutions for high performance computing systems, with plans to buy the rest by 2028.

"While liquid cooling is not a new technology, specific application to the Data Center and AI environment represents a nascent market set for strong growth in the coming years," Schneider (OTCPK:SBGSF) (OTCPK:SBGSY) said.

The company said the data center and networks market accounted for 21% of its 2023 orders, or ~€8B worth of sales, and is seeing double digit growth this year.
